tools.description: Validate one Windows artifact against local offline baselines. Use `type='file'` for a Windows path with optional hash, `type='hash'` for a LOLDrivers vulnerable-driver lookup, `type='filename'` for deception heuristics, `type='lolbin'` for LOLBin context, or `type='dll'` for DLL hijackability. UNKNOWN is neutral: it means not present in the local DB, not evidence of malice. For hash/IOC reputation use `cti_lookup_ioc`. Example: `wintriage_check_artifact(type='file', value='C:\\Windows\\System32\\svchost.exe', os_version='Win10_21H2_Pro')`.

hash.description: Optional file hash when type='file' (baseline mismatch check).
hash.title: Hash

os_version.description: Optional OS filter for type='file' (e.g. Win10_21H2_Pro).
os_version.title: Os Version

type.description: file=path baseline (+optional hash); hash=LOLDrivers vulnerable-driver lookup; filename=deception heuristics; lolbin=LOLBin context; dll=hijackability.
type.title: ArtifactType

value.description: file=Windows path; hash=MD5/SHA1/SHA256; filename/lolbin=filename; dll=DLL name. No null bytes; length-capped per type.
value.title: Value

tools.name: wintriage_check_artifact

filename_in_baseline.description: For type='file': filename exists anywhere in the baseline.
filename_in_baseline.title: Filename In Baseline

is_lolbin.description: True when the filename is a known living-off-the-land binary.
is_lolbin.title: Is Lolbin

is_system_path.description: For type='file': path is under a Windows system directory.
is_system_path.title: Is System Path

path_in_baseline.description: For type='file': exact path exists in the baseline.
path_in_baseline.title: Path In Baseline

subtype_data.description: Type-specific fields such as vulnerable_driver, algorithm, hash, scenarios_by_type, or filename analysis.
subtype_data.title: Subtype Data

tools.title: Check Windows Artifact

tools.description: Validate a parent-to-child Windows process relationship against the local process-tree baseline. It checks never-spawns rules for injection targets, suspicious-parent blacklists such as Office or browsers spawning shells, and valid-parent allowlists such as `svchost.exe` descending from `services.exe`. Use on process-creation evidence. UNKNOWN is neutral when the process is not in the local expectations DB. Example: `wintriage_check_process_tree(process_name='cmd.exe', parent_name='winword.exe')`.

path.description: Optional executable path for tighter matching.
path.title: Path

tools.name: wintriage_check_process_tree

expected_parents.description: Known-good parent process names for this child process.
expected_parents.title: Expected Parents

in_expectations_db.description: True when the child process exists in the local expectations DB.
in_expectations_db.title: In Expectations Db

suspicious_parents.description: Known suspicious parent names for this child process.
suspicious_parents.title: Suspicious Parents

user_context.description: User-context validation details when user was provided.
user_context.title: User Context

tools.title: Check Process Tree

tools.description: Validate Windows persistence and system configuration against OS-version baselines: `type='service'`, `type='scheduled_task'`, or `type='autorun'`. `os_version` is required because services, tasks, and autoruns vary by release. UNKNOWN is neutral unless the result includes concrete suspicious findings. Example: `wintriage_check_system(type='service', name='EventLog', os_version='Win10_21H2_Pro', binary_path='C:\\Windows\\System32\\svchost.exe')`.

name.description: Service name, scheduled-task path, or autorun registry key path.
name.title: Name

os_version.description: Target OS (e.g. Win10_21H2_Pro, W11_22H2, Server2022). REQUIRED because baselines vary by release.
os_version.title: Os Version

type.description: System check type: service, scheduled_task, or autorun.
type.title: SystemType

tools.name: wintriage_check_system

in_baseline.description: True when the service/task/autorun is present in baseline.
in_baseline.title: In Baseline

lookup_performed.description: False only when validation prevented a baseline lookup.
lookup_performed.title: Lookup Performed

os_versions.description: OS versions associated with the match.
os_versions.title: Os Versions

tools.title: Check System Persistence

tools.description: Check a registry key or value against the full Windows registry baseline. This requires the optional large `known_good_registry.db`. Use for general registry validation; for autorun persistence prefer `wintriage_check_system(type='autorun')` because it is faster and does not require the large registry DB. UNKNOWN is neutral: not in the local registry baseline is not evidence of malice. Example: `wintriage_check_registry(key_path='SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run')`.
tools.name: wintriage_check_registry

in_baseline.description: True when the key/value exists in the registry baseline.
in_baseline.title: In Baseline

match_count.description: Total matching registry rows before output capping.
match_count.title: Match Count

os_version_count.description: Total OS-version count before output capping.
os_version_count.title: Os Version Count

os_versions.description: Up to 10 OS versions associated with matching entries.
os_versions.title: Os Versions

value_count.description: Total value count before output capping.
value_count.title: Value Count

tools.title: Check Registry Baseline

tools.description: Check a named pipe against known Windows pipes and known C2 pipe patterns such as Cobalt Strike or Metasploit. SUSPICIOUS means a known C2-pattern match; EXPECTED means a standard Windows pipe; UNKNOWN is neutral when neither catalog matches. Use when named pipe artifacts appear in Sysmon, EDR, process, or memory evidence. Example: `wintriage_check_pipe(pipe_name='\\\\.\\pipe\\msagent_12')`.

pipe_name.description: Named pipe (with or without \\.\pipe\ prefix; normalized before lookup).
pipe_name.title: Pipe Name

tools.name: wintriage_check_pipe

description.description: Pipe context from the local baseline database.
description.title: Description

is_suspicious.description: True when the pipe matches a known C2 pipe pattern.
is_suspicious.title: Is Suspicious

is_windows_pipe.description: True when the pipe is a known standard Windows pipe.
is_windows_pipe.title: Is Windows Pipe

malware_family.description: Malware family associated with suspicious matches.
malware_family.title: Malware Family

protocol.description: Windows protocol associated with expected pipe matches.
protocol.title: Protocol

service_name.description: Windows service associated with expected pipe matches.
service_name.title: Service Name

tool_name.description: C2 tool or framework name for suspicious matches.
tool_name.title: Tool Name

tools.title: Check Named Pipe
